name: Tabs
description: Rounded-rectangle tab row — each tab is a self-contained chip carrying a required leading icon and a label, sharing the Segmented geometry verbatim but swapping the capsule radius for sys.radius.md (8) so the tabs read as soft rounded rectangles instead of pills. Reach for this on feed / list headers where the row of choices should sit as standalone chips with a more contained, card-adjacent silhouette.

onChange.description: Fired with the next value when a tab is selected.

leadingIcon.description: Optional 16px (sys.icon.md) context glyph rendered before the label. May carry its own brand / category color independent of the label.

label.description: Tab's accessible name. Single line. Omit only when the tab is icon-only (pair with an `aria-label` for assistive tech). At least one of label / leadingIcon must be present.

leadingIcon.description: 16px (sys.icon.md) context glyph before the label. At least one of label / leadingIcon must be present.

unselected.label: sys.color.onSurface
unselected.note: Transparent fill so the tab adopts whatever surface sits behind it — page, raised card, sheet — without pinning to a fixed neutral step. Inherited from Filter chip's unselected recipe.

focusIndicator.description: Keyboard-focus visual — an accessibility indicator, not a lifecycle state. Composes over whichever lifecycle state the tab is in. The `states.focused` block above is kept for JSX runtime consumers; this block is the parallel external-reader contract.
focusIndicator.compositionReason: The Tabs row is a horizontal scroller; an outward ring would clip at the row's top/bottom edges.

ring.outerLayerPosition: depth 0..2px from the tab edge (the outer stroke)
ring.insetLayerPosition: depth 2..3px from the tab edge (the counter-ring just inside the outer stroke)
ring.implementation: inset box-shadow on the tab's `::after` overlay. Constrained strictly inside the tab's footprint and never exceeds it.

focusIndicator.trigger: :focus-visible (keyboard / programmatic focus, never plain mouse click)
